description: Lifecycle case (model-training effort) with a reversed decision and a ruled-out/deprecated item; the chat contains the superseded content, the live state does not.

chat.content: We're picking the architecture for the churn model. Thoughts?
Start with gradient boosting (XGBoost); it's strong on tabular churn data.
We trained XGBoost and a small MLP. The MLP beat it by 4 AUC points on the holdout.
Then switch: the decision is to use the MLP, not XGBoost. XGBoost is dropped as the primary model.
Someone said our gains came from just adding more training data. True?
No. We ruled that out — adding more rows didn't move AUC; the gain came from fixing label noise in the churn definition.
What's the hard limit on training?
Initially one GPU. But after the MLP, we relaxed it: two GPUs are approved for training runs.
Yes, open: the ML team wants real-time serving, the platform team wants nightly batch scoring. Unresolved.
Whether to quantize the MLP to int8 for serving — undecided. So: MLP chosen, label-noise fix is the real driver, two GPUs allowed, serving conflict and quantization open.
